personas.name: Noor, an Overwhelmed Professional
personas.description: Noor uses Calm when stress builds up during the day and she needs quick emotional relief. She is not looking for a complex wellness routine; she wants fast, dependable support when she feels anxious, panicked, or emotionally overwhelmed.
goals: Reduce anxiety and emotional overwhelm in the moment
Use short calming activities that fit into a busy day
Feel mentally grounded during stressful periods
pain_points: Stress and anxiety escalate quickly during work or personal life
Needs support that works immediately during panic or overwhelm
Has limited time and cannot commit to long sessions
context: Uses Calm during stressful workdays, anxious evenings, or moments of panic
Often turns to breathing exercises, guided meditations, or short calming sessions
May already be dealing with ongoing stress, anxiety, or emotional exhaustion
constraints: Support content must be quick to access and effective in a short time
The experience should feel soothing rather than mentally demanding
The app should be reliable during moments of high stress

personas.name: Ibrahim a Cautious Subscriber
personas.description: Ibrahim is interested in Calm's content, but he is very sensitive to pricing, subscriptions, and free trial terms. He becomes frustrated when the app feels misleading, especially when most content is locked or when billing and cancellation are unclear.
goals: Try the app before committing financially
Understand exactly what is free and what is paid
Avoid unwanted charges and cancel easily if needed
pain_points: Feels misled by free trial and subscription flows
Finds too much content locked behind a paywall
Has difficulty understanding or stopping recurring charges
context: Usually starts with the free version or a trial before deciding whether to subscribe
Checks pricing and value carefully before paying for wellness apps
May uninstall or leave negative feedback after billing confusion
constraints: Pricing, trial duration, and cancellation steps must be transparent
Some meaningful content should be accessible before payment
The subscription flow should not create surprise charges

personas.name: Zain an Offline Listener
personas.description: Zain depends on Calm in places where internet access is weak, unavailable, or inconvenient, especially at night or while traveling. He downloads content in advance and expects it to play smoothly offline, so failures in offline mode make the app feel unreliable.
goals: Access downloaded audio without an internet connection
Use sleep stories and meditations while traveling or offline
Rely on previously downloaded content without interruption
pain_points: Downloaded sessions fail or disappear in offline mode
Playback errors interrupt sleep or relaxation routines
Feels premium features are unreliable despite paying for the app
context: Uses Calm on airplanes, during travel, in low connectivity areas, or while sleeping
Often downloads content ahead of time expecting seamless offline playback
May use the app at bedtime and does not want the phone connected to the internet
constraints: Downloaded content must remain accessible offline
Offline playback should be stable and predictable
The app should not require internet access for already downloaded sessions

personas.name: Sobia a frustrated user
personas.description: Sobia believes Calm has useful content, but she struggles to find what she needs when she needs it. She often opens the app already tired, stressed, or trying to sleep, so a cluttered and confusing interface makes the experience worse instead of calming.
goals: Find the right meditation, soundscape, or sleep story quickly
Return easily to favourite or already in progress content
Use the app without added frustration or confusion
pain_points: Search and navigation feel cluttered and disorganized
Important content is hard to find or buried under recommendations
The interface increases stress instead of reducing it
context: Often uses the app at bedtime or in moments of distress or when already mentally overloaded
May return repeatedly to the same content and wants easy access to it
Is especially sensitive to confusing menus, poor categorization, and distracting interface elements
constraints: Navigation must be simple and predictable
Search and categorization should help users find content fast
The interface should reduce cognitive load during stressful moments

personas.name: Mubarak a tired father
personas.description: Mubarak uses Calm as part of his nightly wind down routine. He values soothing audio such as sleep stories, music, soundscapes, and guided meditations because they help him relax, fall asleep faster, and settle a worried mind at the end of the day.
goals: Fall asleep more easily and sleep more peacefully
Use calming audio to unwind at night
Manage stress and mental restlessness through soothing content
pain_points: Struggles to relax or fall asleep when stressed or anxious
Needs audio content that is consistently soothing and easy to start
Relies on bedtime content as part of a regular sleep routine
context: Usually opens Calm at night, before sleep, or after waking during the night
Frequently uses sleep stories, relaxing music, and soundscapes
May also use the app during the day for quick relaxation or emotional reset
constraints: Audio content must be easy to start and pleasant to listen to
The experience should support uninterrupted bedtime use
Sleep and relaxation features should feel dependable enough for daily routines
